cards.back: A cipher used by the Republican Party to secretly communicate with supporters in the 1876 U.S. presidential election
A cipher used by the Republican Party to communicate with supporters in the 1876 U.S. presidential election, supposedly created by James A. Blaine
A cipher allegedly used by the Democratic Party to secretly communicate with supporters in the 1876 U.S. presidential election
A cipher reportedly used by Horace Greeley, the Democratic Party's presidential candidate in the 1876 U.S. presidential election
The 14th and 15th Amendments to the U.S. Constitution, passed in 1868 and 1870, respectively, which aimed to redefine citizenship and voting rights after the Civil War
